task.title: How to Download CapCut and Start a New Project
task.shortdesc: Learn how to download CapCut and create your first editing project. Completing this task will prepare you to begin editing videos for social media.

taskbody.prereq:
Before starting
,
make sure you have an iPhone or iPad with internet access and enough storage space to install applications.
taskbody.context: Before editing videos in CapCut, users must first download the application and create a project. Creating a project allows users to import video clips and begin organizing footage for editing.

step.cmd: Open the App Store on your iPhone or iPad.
step.info: The App Store icon is usually located on the home screen.
step.stepresult: The App Store home page appears.

step.cmd: Search for "CapCut" using the search bar.
step.info:
Make sure to select the official CapCut application
.

step.cmd: Tap Get or Download to install the application.
step.info: The app may require Face ID, Touch ID, or an Apple ID password before downloading.
step.stepresult: CapCut downloads and appears on your device home screen.

step.info: When opening the app for the first time, CapCut may request access to photos and videos.
This option starts a new editing project and allows you to select media files.
step.stepresult: The photo and video library opens.

step.cmd: Select the video clips or photos you want to edit.
step.info: Choose clips in the order you want them to appear in your video.

choices.choice: • Select photos if creating a slideshow-style video.

step.stepresult: Your clips appear in the editing timeline and the project is ready for editing.

taskbody.result: You have successfully downloaded CapCut and created a new editing project. Your media is now ready to be arranged and edited.
taskbody.postreq: Continue to How to Import, Arrange, and Trim Video Clips to begin organizing your footage.
